title: Fix mk2-alteration-gate: remove shallow fetch depth=1 causing no-merge-base failure

verification.plan: 1. Remove --depth=1 from 'git fetch origin main' in the Fetch base step; the prior actions/checkout with fetch-depth: 0 already provides full history and the shallow re-fetch overwrites it, preventing merge-base computation. 2. Switch MK2_BASE_REF from origin/<branch-name> to github.event.pull_request.base.sha for a stable, immutable reference. 3. Emit debug logging (base ref/SHA, git rev-parse, git merge-base) before running the gate so future failures are easier to diagnose. 4. The merge-base command acts as a fail-closed guard: if no merge base is computable the step exits non-zero and the job fails. 5. Validate workflow YAML syntax. 6. Confirm the alteration gate self-check passes with the new workflow.

evidence.notes: Root cause: actions/checkout fetches all branches with full history (fetch-depth: 0), but the subsequent 'git fetch origin main --depth=1' converts origin/main to a shallow commit, stripping its parent history. The three-dot diff 'origin/main...HEAD' requires git to walk back to find a common ancestor; with a shallow origin/main that walk terminates immediately at the shallow boundary, producing 'fatal: origin/main...HEAD: no merge base'. Fix: remove --depth=1 so the full-history fetch from checkout is preserved. Using the PR base SHA (github.event.pull_request.base.sha) instead of the branch name makes the diff stable even if the branch advances between checkout and gate execution.
